input.title: Security Operations and Incident Response Platform
input.description: Security Operations and Incident Response Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Python FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Security Alert Ingestion
requirements: Users must be able to complete security alert ingestion through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing security alert ingestion to continue.
Security Alert Ingestion must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for security alert ingestion should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-02.title: Incident Case Creation
requirements: The system must support incident case creation as a complete business workflow within the application.
Incident Case Creation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with incident case creation from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to incident case creation for operational support.

FR-03.title: Severity Classification Workflow
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete severity classification workflow according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving severity classification workflow changes.
Severity Classification Workflow must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for severity classification workflow.

FR-04.title: Analyst Assignment Queue
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete analyst assignment queue according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving analyst assignment queue changes.
Analyst Assignment Queue must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for analyst assignment queue.

FR-05.title: Investigation Timeline Tracking
requirements: The system must capture investigation timeline tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Investigation Timeline Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile investigation timeline tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during investigation timeline tracking.

FR-06.title: Evidence Attachment Repository
requirements: The system must support evidence attachment repository as a complete business workflow within the application.
Evidence Attachment Repository should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with evidence attachment repository from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to evidence attachment repository for operational support.

FR-07.title: Indicator of Compromise Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end indicator of compromise management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Indicator of Compromise Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to indicator of compromise management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within indicator of compromise management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-08.title: Containment Action Approval
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete containment action approval according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving containment action approval changes.
Containment Action Approval must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for containment action approval.

FR-09.title: Endpoint Isolation Request Workflow
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete endpoint isolation request workflow according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving endpoint isolation request workflow changes.
Endpoint Isolation Request Workflow must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for endpoint isolation request workflow.

FR-10.title: Threat Intelligence Feed Correlation
requirements: The platform must support threat intelligence feed correlation through stable service contracts with external systems or internal integration layers.
Threat Intelligence Feed Correlation should validate inbound and outbound payloads before data is accepted or shared with downstream systems.
Failures, retries, and reconciliation outcomes for threat intelligence feed correlation must be observable through logs, alerts, and operational dashboards.
Credentials, tokens, and connection settings used by threat intelligence feed correlation must be managed securely and rotated according to policy.

FR-11.title: SLA Breach Alerting
requirements: The system must trigger sla breach alerting when the relevant business events or threshold conditions occur.
Users should be able to configure delivery channels, timing, or opt-in preferences related to sla breach alerting.
SLA Breach Alerting must record delivery outcomes, retries, and failures for operational follow-up.
The interface should let authorized users review recent sla breach alerting events and the current notification status.

FR-12.title: Executive Incident Summary Dashboard
requirements: The system must provide executive incident summary dashboard with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.
Executive Incident Summary Dashboard should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records.
Authorized users must be able to export or share executive incident summary dashboard outputs in formats used by business teams.
The platform should retain query history, report configuration, or dashboard preferences where that improves repeated analysis.

FR-13.title: Root Cause Documentation
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end root cause documentation capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Root Cause Documentation should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to root cause documentation without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within root cause documentation and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-14.title: Post-incident Review Tracking
requirements: The system must capture post-incident review tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Post-incident Review Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile post-incident review tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during post-incident review tracking.

FR-15.title: Audit-ready Incident Export
requirements: The platform must support audit-ready incident export through stable service contracts with external systems or internal integration layers.
Audit-ready Incident Export should validate inbound and outbound payloads before data is accepted or shared with downstream systems.
Failures, retries, and reconciliation outcomes for audit-ready incident export must be observable through logs, alerts, and operational dashboards.
Credentials, tokens, and connection settings used by audit-ready incident export must be managed securely and rotated according to policy.

FR-16.title: Role-based Access for Security Teams
requirements: Users must be able to complete role-based access for security teams through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing role-based access for security teams to continue.
Role-based Access for Security Teams must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for role-based access for security teams should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-17.title: Secure Notification Escalation
requirements: The system must trigger secure notification escalation when the relevant business events or threshold conditions occur.
Users should be able to configure delivery channels, timing, or opt-in preferences related to secure notification escalation.
Secure Notification Escalation must record delivery outcomes, retries, and failures for operational follow-up.
The interface should let authorized users review recent secure notification escalation events and the current notification status.

FR-18.title: Playbook Runbook Catalog
requirements: The system must capture playbook runbook catalog data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Playbook Runbook Catalog should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile playbook runbook catalog records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during playbook runbook catalog.

FR-01.title: Design Security Alert Ingestion UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for security alert ingestion. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for security alert ingestion is complete and reviewable.
The security alert ingestion design is responsive and accessible.
The design for security alert ingestion aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Security Alert Ingestion
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for security alert ingestion. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Users must be able to complete security alert ingestion through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing security alert ingestion to continue; Security Alert Ingestion must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required security alert ingestion user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for security alert ingestion are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for security alert ingestion is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Security Alert Ingestion
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for security alert ingestion. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Users must be able to complete security alert ingestion through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing security alert ingestion to continue; Security Alert Ingestion must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for security alert ingestion is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for security alert ingestion enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for security alert ingestion is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Security Alert Ingestion
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for security alert ingestion. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for security alert ingestion enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for security alert ingestion are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of security alert ingestion satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: DevOps Setup for Security Alert Ingestion
FR-01.description: Set up the operational support for security alert ingestion. Configure deployment settings, secrets, background jobs, monitoring, alerting, backup, restore, or recovery controls needed for the feature to run safely in target environments.
acceptance_criteria: Operational setup for security alert ingestion covers deployment, configuration, and environment readiness.
Monitoring, alerting, backup, or recovery controls relevant to security alert ingestion are in place and verifiable.
The runtime behavior of security alert ingestion can be observed, supported, and recovered in target environments.

FR-01.title: Testing Security Alert Ingestion Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for security alert ingestion. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for security alert ingestion.
Security, validation, and permission checks for security alert ingestion are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important security alert ingestion behaviors.

FR-16.title: Frontend Implementation of Role-based Access for Security Teams
FR-16.description: Implement the frontend behavior for role-based access for security teams. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Users must be able to complete role-based access for security teams through the approved entry points provided by the platform; The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing role-based access for security teams to continue; Role-based Access for Security Teams must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
